title: Rich Flaky Uruguayan Cheese Empanadas
description: A salty, velvety Uruguayan dish featuring sazon and a lamination-puff pastry, perfect for a lazy Sunday, and ovo-vegetarian-friendly. This recipe yields a delicious and flaky empanada filled with melted cheese.

ingredients.name: grated cheese
ingredients.notes: such as queso fresco or mozzarella

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour and salt, then add cold butter and use a pastry blender or fingers to work it into the flour until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add water to the bowl, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the chilled dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Use a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon of grated cheese in the center of each dough circle, then sprinkle with sazon seasoning.
Fold the dough in half over the filling, pressing edges to seal.
Use a fork to crimp the edges and create a decorative border.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with an egg wash.
Bake the empanadas in a preheated oven at 375 degrees for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
